Where was I talking about the rest of the heart story? You gave me the 26 grand or 27 I gave You don't want to go to start so I I paid them $26,000 and We moved to Lexington my wife loved Lexington and About two months later I I Get a Medicare card I Never applied for a Medicare card So I called them first place. I thought you got Medicare when you got 65. I was 62 I'm telling you this so that you'll know I didn't know So I called Medicare and I say you just sent me a Medicare card. I shouldn't be calling it, but I got to know How come I'm only 62 Said let me look so she looked it up and she said you have applied for disability insurance if you have disability insurance for I think it Was six months you automatically get a disability Medicare card. I Didn't know that So I took the medic the card Into the hospital my Medicare card Now the first thing you have to know is I have now in six months Know know everybody in that hospital by their first name Especially the guy with deals with the money So I went into her and I said I just got this in the mail Medicare card and I put it on her desk And I said could I get my $26,000 back? And She says I don't know So let me get do some telephone calls and then come out back in the morning. I went back in the morning And she said yes, you get your $26,000 back your insurance will pay for everything that they don't pay for and The insurance will pay it all Now you know that it was your hospital or my hospital at least it was my hospital I would have said mr. Douglas We're gonna have some extra expenses maybe and Would you mind if we keep the $26,000 to keep so that if we do have any cost at Medicare won't pay You'll pay what would I said? It was not a personal hospital it was owned by a state was a part of a of a of a You know it's a Kentucky hospital so anything that's part of government doesn't give a damn I Wouldn't have given your money back I'd have probably said mr. Douglas, you know in truth if he gave us another 26 it really make us feel better All right, so get to the heart So get to the heart So we went there and we Took the test every month They put a beeper on me and said that I could not I had to live within 30 minutes of the hospital So I owned a 87 foot actual paddle boat And we moved the paddle boat to Kentucky up the Kentucky River Ohio River to Kentucky River and I lived on my paddle boat while I was waiting for my heart And So I had a captain on the paddle boat So the captain and I and my wife went to dinner at a Japanese restaurant It took them a year to find my heart instead of six months and They called me and they said we have a heart for you Except it is by a drug dealer The reason we're offering you the heart I was 60 Two or three or four five at the time We're offering you the heart because nobody else wants to take a chance of taking it We've done tests and we don't think there's anything there, but we can't always be sure So you are taking a chance Then I said that a doctor Doctor, what would you do if it was you were you were me? He said I'd take it you're getting kind of old They don't like to give them over 60 Because they'd rather give the heart to a younger person and so what I read or give I would have died and let a younger man Have it definitely I said fine with me give it to them So I We were at working we were eating Dick or Jack and Jeannie went and got in the car And I walked out to the car And I remember getting in the car And after that I'm blank Remember my mother told you I told you my mother taught me how to eliminate all all things bad I don't remember anything the only thing I remember is waking up two weeks later after I got my heart and Jack is in the bed and my my captain who it became my dearest friend. He just died of cancer Jack was in the hospital bed with me with that next to me I don't know how my wife did it. She still won't tell me she got Jack There's a double room and he stayed with me Jack's theory is and I'm telling you never go in the hospital alone Have someone there 24 hours a day checking all medicines because all is dealing with you It's humans just like you and me and we all make mistakes and you've got to check on so Jack checked everything and I was in the hospital for about I don't know three or four months and one morning I woke up and he said Jack said you're down to a hundred and forty two pounds I want what went at a hundred and ninety five He said not feeding you enough and I've got the gal coming up when she came up and I couldn't eat they fed me Intervenously, I whatever you call it and He said you're not getting fed enough and so she came up and they doubled my feet Now why the doctor he looked at that chart every morning when he came in didn't he look at my weight. I Don't know I still don't understand So anyway, that's what he caught that was his story
